name: Pick One Word per Concept
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 2: Meaningful Names
problem: The same abstract operation is named differently across classes (e.g., fetch, retrieve, get), forcing developers to remember which synonym belongs to which class and making the codebase inconsistent.
steps: Establish a single verb for each abstract operation and apply it consistently across all classes (e.g., always use get, never mix with fetch or retrieve).
Build a team-wide lexicon or glossary for domain concepts.
Use the same noun for the same concept across the codebase.
Audit method names across classes with similar responsibilities and rename for consistency.
Avoid multiple controller/manager/driver names for things that serve the same role.
